notes: Opening opportunity openings:plaintiff. Inspected current record with get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence. Only visible evidence is case-packet ev_90fa205907f7_market-page, sha256 90fa205907f7af6483463b7dcda6e06a38a2e22fbe19312319fdf46a78f14646, text/plain, 4641 bytes. Read full contents via read_evidence_range. Packet is a Polymarket market record captured 2026-05-12, not primary proof. It preserves market rule: Yes if any message/note written by Epstein and intended as suicide note is publicly available by listed date; qualifying note must be credibly reported to have been written by Epstein and intended as suicide note/final message/equivalent; public release can be official/leaked/otherwise; resolution source consensus of credible reporting. Packet says platform context reported a U.S. District Judge in New York unsealed a purported suicide note attributed to Epstein on May 7, 2026, but flags authenticity and intent doubts. Market at issue is May 8 market id 2128535 slug epstein-suicide-note-released-by-may-8; UMA status disputed; page showed competing proposed outcomes/final review. Source map: decisive elements are public availability by May 8 23:59 ET, credible reporting, attribution to Epstein, intent as suicide/final message/equivalent, and whether authenticity/intent doubts defeat only weight rather than qualification. Native web searches performed: "Epstein suicide note" "May 7, 2026" unsealed; "Jeffrey Epstein" "suicide note" "May 7" "unsealed"; site:storage.courtlistener.com Epstein suicide note unsealed May 7 2026; "Epstein suicide note released by May 8"; "Nicholas Tartaglione" "Epstein" "suicide note" PDF court May 2026; "United States v. Tartaglione" "Epstein" "suicide note" "Karas"; "Epstein" "Tartaglione" "suicide note" "courtlistener". Leads found for later admissible evidence: SDNY order PDF at https://www.nysd.uscourts.gov/sites/default/files/2026-05/16cr832%20-%20Tartaglione%20Order%20-%2026.5.6.pdf; Courthouse News NYT letter PDF; CourtListener docket United States v. Tartaglione 7:16-cr-00832-KMK; AP/WSJ/Washington Post/Guardian/Time/Axios reports. Adverse issues: DOJ reportedly did not authenticate or previously see the note; unsigned note; custody chain through Nicholas Tartaglione, an interested former cellmate and convicted murderer; Epstein initially accused Tartaglione; family/others reportedly dispute authenticity; 'purported' language and exact intent remain contested. Opening theory: plaintiff should not claim the underlying note is proven by the current record alone. Opening should say the record identifies the correct rule, deadline, and source target; plaintiff expects to prove via court order/note plus credible reporting that the rule's credible-reporting/public-availability standard is met. Work journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant opening work notes for arb-ex08a-20260630051012. Opportunity openings:defendant; final filing action record_opening_statement only; evidence submission not allowed. Inspected get_case and list_evidence before filing. Current visible record: one case-packet item ev_90fa205907f7_market-page, text/plain, 4641 bytes, sha256 90fa205907f7af6483463b7dcda6e06a38a2e22fbe19312319fdf46a78f14646; plaintiff opening only; no admitted outside source evidence. Used stat_evidence and read_evidence_range to read the packet. Packet proves market metadata and rule text: qualifying note/message must be written by Jeffrey Epstein, intended as suicide note/final message/equivalent, made publicly available by May 8 deadline; resolution source consensus of credible reporting. Packet says Polymarket context captured 2026-05-12 stated a U.S. District Judge in New York unsealed a purported suicide note attributed to Epstein on May 7, 2026, but also noted authenticity and intent doubts; packet says this platform context is market metadata, not independent proof. May 8 market metadata includes market id 2128535, UMA status disputed, captured outcome prices Yes .0005 / No .9995, page visible statuses proposed/disputed/final review. Source map: decisive elements are public availability by deadline, credible reporting, actual authorship attribution, intent as suicide/final/equivalent communication. Strongest primary target for later evidence phase: SDNY/Tartaglione docket, order/endorsed letter, unsealed note image or PDF, docket attachments. Confirming sources: credible news reports before deadline. Adverse targets: lack of official authentication, unsigned note, chain from Tartaglione/counsel/court, whether the note was tied to a prior alleged attempt rather than a final suicide message, and family/official contrary statements. Native web searches run: exact phrases for 'Epstein suicide note' 'May 7 2026' 'unsealed'; 'Tartaglione' 'Karas' docket; 'They investigated me for months FOUND NOTHING'. Leads found include CourtListener docket listing for U.S. v. Tartaglione, Washington Post/Time/WSJ/PBS/BI/UPI/Fox/Axios/FT-style reporting. Adverse leads from snippets include no official agency confirmation and family forgery claim. CourtListener open through web had no usable content in this turn; later evidence phase should fetch docket/RECAP artifacts directly if possible. Draft theory: defense should not deny that public reports may exist; instead argue plaintiff must prove all rule elements, and the current record only identifies a disputed market and a source path. A 'purported' note plus platform summary cannot alone carry written-by-Epstein and intended-as-suicide/final-message. Opening should be forward-looking and preserve evidence plan without citing outside leads as admitted proof.

Merits opportunity arguments:plaintiff. Re-scanned record with get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence before filing; only original case packet plus openings were present, no submitted evidence. Existing packet unchanged: ev_90fa205907f7_market-page sha256 90fa205907f7af6483463b7dcda6e06a38a2e22fbe19312319fdf46a78f14646. Investigation: downloaded SDNY order from official nysd.uscourts.gov URL to /tmp/tartaglione-order.pdf; size 128992, sha256 7225441cb802084e2b9485ebc2c6d246fbfa08e109fe650cb14f445dc4745ca0. Local utilities file/rg/pdftotext missing; python pip unavailable, so used web PDF extraction for readable text. Direct submit_evidence for PDF failed once because shell substitution was passed literally as base64; began upload upl_1541d851b7c6cc5bd657a635 but did not commit binary because transferring chunk base64 was impractical within time and text extract was accepted. Axios direct capture failed HTTP 403 and was not relied on. Downloaded AP HTML from canonical AP URL; size 900481; sha256 bb98febbef9b9534f5e6796ec15425bbc45882e9f639f60a373f376dfadc0337; extracted metadata/body showing article published May 7 2026, note made public, DOJ had not authenticated/seen it, and AP image caption that document was released Thursday May 7 2026 by SDNY. Downloaded Guardian HTML from May 6 article; browser/web extraction showed title, publication before deadline, first time made public, note text, no signature, and Guardian non-verification. Downloaded AP note image; size 839101; sha256 a0a8159ddf477f6fd51bfa0f72b05ce0b94399e5e3b0655fcfa5de8794246823; not submitted due upload/time and because text evidence preserved decisive content. Accepted evidence: ev_1de721f5d505_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-1de721f5d505 SDNY order extract; ev_78537bb92f69_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-78537bb92f69 Guardian report; ev_ebfed5a9aeb0_submitted-evidence-03-plaintiff-ebfed5a9aeb0 AP report/adverse caveat. Merits theory: market packet proves rule and deadline; SDNY proves official court unsealing/docketing before deadline; Guardian proves public reporting before deadline that alleged suicide note written by Epstein was unsealed, first made public, includes final-message-like text and caveats; AP corroborates public availability by May 7 and supplies strongest adverse caveat. Argue credible reporting standard does not require official authentication; doubts go to weight. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant merits work notes. Opportunity arguments:defendant; final action submit_argument; evidence submission allowed. Record inspection performed with get_case/list_evidence. New plaintiff evidence: ev_1de721f5d505 SDNY order extract, ev_78537bb92f69 Guardian article extract, ev_ebfed5a9aeb0 AP article extract, plus packet ev_90fa205907f7. Read exact byte ranges for all four. Evidence analysis: SDNY order proves Judge Karas granted public access/unsealing/docketing for a document described as alleged suicide note purportedly authored by Epstein, recovered by Tartaglione, reportedly submitted by Tartaglione lawyers. It does not make an authenticity finding; public-access reasoning says note was relevant to Curcio hearing and sealing was not justified. Guardian proves pre-deadline reporting, note text, and title/standfirst calling it alleged suicide note written by Epstein, but includes no signature, Guardian non-verification, Tartaglione custody, Epstein accusation of Tartaglione, and Epstein not-suicidal statements. AP proves pre-deadline wire reporting and public availability by May 7, plus strongest caveats: DOJ never had the note, not yet authenticated, first time DOJ saw it, discovered by Tartaglione and given to lawyers to protect himself. Defense native investigation: searched/opened Business Insider Mark Epstein article. It was accessible via web open. Submitted concise extract as ev_b3d357bbd988_submitted-evidence-04-defendant-b3d357bbd988. BI timestamp 2026-05-08T20:44:17.764Z (pre-deadline). BI reports Mark Epstein said note was forgery, could have copied his brother's voice from public emails, and doubted Tartaglione had/found it; also reports note absent from DOJ Epstein files and 128-page DOJ IG report; reports disputed first attempt facts. BI also contains contrary/adverse-to-defense facts: note sealed in May 2021 before public release of Little Rascals emails, timeline shows multiple people tried to authenticate in 2019/2020. Additional web search surfaced post-deadline AP handwriting-expert article favorable to plaintiff, but not submitted because the key market deadline is May 8 and it was not needed for defendant merits; note as adverse lead for later if raised. Legal theory: concede public availability/timing likely shown, but argue proposition requires credible reporting that the note was written by Epstein and intended as suicide/final/equivalent, not merely credible reporting that a purported/alleged note surfaced. Preponderance not met due to unsigned note, no official authentication, DOJ non-possession/non-authentication, OIG absence, Tartaglione conflict/custody, family forgery claim, and ambiguous intent tied to first alleged attempt rather than final message. Filing plan: offer DX-1 BI evidence; discuss PX-2/PX-3/PX-4 and market packet as record evidence; include technical report search ledger.

Rebuttal opportunity rebuttals:plaintiff. Re-scanned case with get_case/list_evidence and stat_evidence for defendant's new BI evidence ev_b3d357bbd988_submitted-evidence-04-defendant-b3d357bbd988. Read full exact bytes with read_evidence_range. Defendant argument concedes plaintiff probably showed document was unsealed/reported by May 6-7; defense attacks authorship and intent. New BI extract says Mark Epstein believed note was a forgery; also says note not in DOJ files/OIG report; also preserves contrary points: note was originally sealed in May 2021 before similar public emails became public in 2026, and a timeline showed the note passed through multiple people who tried to authenticate it in 2019/2020. Analysis: Mark Epstein claim is an interested-family opinion, not expert authentication. Absence from DOJ/OIG and DOJ non-authentication are adverse but do not control a rule requiring credible reporting, not official authentication. No new evidence needed; existing PX-1 through PX-4 and defense BI extract are sufficient. Rebuttal should be narrow: credible reporting standard, BI's internal plaintiff-friendly fact, and intent supported by note text/context. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant surrebuttal notes. Opportunity surrebuttals:defendant. Record inspection performed with get_case and list_evidence. No new evidence beyond existing five items; plaintiff rebuttal visible. Plaintiff rebuttal's strongest points: defense conceded public release/reporting by May 6-7; credible reporting does not require official authentication; Business Insider copycat theory is weakened by the article's own 2021 seal chronology; intent is satisfied by the context of a first suspected suicide attempt plus text about choosing one's time to say goodbye and 'NO FUN - NOT WORTH IT.' Analysis: no targeted new outside search needed because rebuttal raises legal/weight issues based on existing evidence, not a new factual source. Defense response should be narrow: public availability is not enough; the rule requires credible reporting as to authorship and intent; plaintiff sources repeatedly say alleged/purported/attributed/not verified/not authenticated/DOJ never had it. BI chronology point is mixed and acknowledged, but it does not supply affirmative handwriting proof. DX-1 remains useful for DOJ/OIG absence and Mark Epstein's pre-deadline forgery claim. Intent remains contested because the note was tied to a disputed July incident, not immediately before death, and was preserved in Tartaglione's unrelated criminal case with an alternate litigation purpose. Plan: file concise surrebuttal, no new technical report needed.

Closing opportunity closings:plaintiff. Final record scan completed with get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence. No new evidence after defendant BI extract; defendant surrebuttal argues public release/reporting is conceded but authorship/intent remain unproven, emphasizing hedging language, DOJ non-authentication, BI/Mark Epstein forgery view, and ambiguity of text. Closing plan: decide under preponderance and market rule, not courtroom authentication. Public availability by deadline is conceded and independently shown by SDNY order, Guardian, AP. Credible reporting element satisfied by court source plus Guardian/AP before May 8 with caveats disclosed. Intent/final-message-equivalent supported by Guardian note text and July suicide-attempt context. Best adverse point remains no official authentication/unsigned/Mark Epstein claim; response is that this prevents certainty, not preponderance, and BI includes facts weakening copycat theory. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant closing notes. Opportunity closings:defendant. Final filing action deliver_closing_statement only; no evidence or technical reports allowed. Required scan completed with get_case and list_evidence. Evidence list unchanged: market packet, plaintiff SDNY order extract, plaintiff Guardian extract, plaintiff AP extract, defendant BI extract. Plaintiff closing now in record; it concedes caveats but argues credible reporting plus court release and context satisfies preponderance. Closing strategy: concede public release/reporting before May 8 because the record supports it, but keep focus on the proposition's full elements. Official court action was public access only, not an authenticity or intent finding. Guardian/AP are credible outlets but the admitted extracts repeatedly hedge authorship and intent: alleged/purported/attributed, unsigned, not Guardian-verified, DOJ never had it, DOJ had not authenticated it and was seeing it for first time, chain through Tartaglione/lawyers. BI DX-1 does not conclusively disprove, but adds pre-deadline forgery claim, absence from DOJ files and OIG report, and first-incident dispute. Defense closing should argue the record proves a publicly released alleged note, not by preponderance a qualifying note credibly reported as actually written and intended by Epstein as suicide/final/equivalent. No new outside investigation needed/allowed for closing beyond existing record synthesis.
